2026-03-06.thesis: Iran blocks Strait of Hormuz, tanker rates hit record
2026-03-06.exit_case: none — Strait of Hormuz remains actively blocked, tanker rates still at record highs
2026-03-06.assessment: Entry week. Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz since Feb 28 following US-Israeli strikes has driven VLCC rates to an all-time record of $423,736/day. This is a clean, datable, acute chokepoint shock with a direct pure-play vehicle (BWET) still described as 'under-owned.' Catalyst is fresh and ongoing — no resolution yet, no reopening, no ceasefire. Conviction high given the acute, specific, still-escalating nature of the crisis and the explicit tie to tanker economics.
2026-03-06.exit_advice: Hold BWET while Hormuz remains blocked and VLCC rates stay at record levels; exit if strait reopens or rates normalize sharply.

2026-04-03.thesis: Iran blocks Strait of Hormuz, tanker rates hit record
2026-04-03.exit_case: none — Trump reportedly willing to end war WITHOUT reopening Hormuz, meaning blockade persists even if war ends
2026-04-03.assessment: Fourth week update: this is the first direct, on-topic news since entry. Report that Trump is willing to end the war WITHOUT reopening Hormuz is critical — it explicitly confirms the driving condition (Strait blockage, and by extension record VLCC rates) is NOT contingent on a ceasefire and may persist independently. This is fresh, relevant coverage (not off-topic macro noise like prior weeks), so conviction resets upward from the silence-decayed level, though not to entry-week highs given the news is indirect (no explicit rate/BWET figures) and introduces some uncertainty about war trajectory. No reversal, reopening, or restored shipping flow reported — the reverse: the blockade is framed as durable/separable from war outcome. Thesis remains live and the pure-play vehicle intact.
2026-04-03.exit_advice: Hold BWET; blockade condition explicitly confirmed as separate from war resolution, so record tanker rates likely persist even if broader conflict winds down.

2026-04-10.thesis: Iran blocks Strait of Hormuz, tanker rates hit record
2026-04-10.exit_case: US-Iran agreed two-week ceasefire; markets rallying on expectations Strait of Hormuz reopens, crude falls.
2026-04-10.assessment: After weeks of silence, this week brought decisive on-topic news: a Trump deadline for Iran to reopen Hormuz was followed almost immediately by a US-Iran ceasefire agreement, explicitly tied by markets to expectations that Strait energy flows resume (crude fell, global equities rallied on the news). This is a direct reversal of the acute driving condition that justified BWET's record-VLCC-rate thesis — unlike last week's report that Trump might accept a war-end without reopening Hormuz, this week's ceasefire is framed with explicit reopening expectations. Given the binary nature of the acute chokepoint catalyst (blocked vs. not blocked), a ceasefire with reopening expectations is a resolution signal, not mere quiet. Tanker rate premium built on blockade scarcity should compress rapidly regardless of whether the ceasefire ultimately holds long-term, since the market prices forward expectations. Thesis is no longer live; cull from shortlist.
2026-04-10.exit_advice: Exit BWET — the driving condition (Hormuz blockade) has reversed via a negotiated ceasefire, with markets already pricing restored Strait flow and falling crude/tanker economics; the acute chokepoint thesis is broken even if the ceasefire proves temporary.
